candidates.direction_name: Skip The Final No-Refill CTA Sync On The Compact PTX Anchor
ranking_notes: This is the highest-signal next move because it isolates one measurable barrier seam on the accepted anchor without reopening any broader family.
The edit is small enough to keep the checkpoint round interpretable even if the gain is modest.
If this does not move the 24.68 ms base, the compact PTX barrier family is likely close to exhausted.
candidates.hypothesis: On the penultimate tile, the current compact PTX loop still pays cp_async_wait_group_0 plus __syncthreads even though future_tile_idx is already out of range and no shared stage will be overwritten. Skipping that final no-refill sync should remove one CTA barrier from the steady-state path without changing registers, stage depth, or the shared-memory budget.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: The remaining local tax is unnecessary final-drain barrier overhead on the accepted compact PTX surface.
candidates.risk: Low-to-moderate. This is a tiny control-path trim, but it still changes CTA synchronization semantics on the last steady-state handoff.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if removing the final sync causes any correctness mismatch or if the change grows into a broader steady-state refactor.
expected_local_changes: Special-case the penultimate-tile handoff so the loop skips the final no-refill __syncthreads.
Keep the compact 128x128 PTX dispatch, grouped_rows=4, and 22,016 B shared-memory footprint unchanged.
Do not combine this trim with any wait_group_1 or refill-before-wait rewrite.

candidates.direction_name: Keep The Guarded 2-K-Stage Pg2s Port As The Broader Fallback
ranking_notes: This remains the broader fallback once the compact barrier seam is squeezed further.
It ranks second because the checkpoint round should prefer a smaller, more interpretable experiment first.
If the final-drain trim is flat, this family becomes more attractive again.
candidates.hypothesis: The 2-K-stage Pg2s family still targets latency hiding, but it is broader and less interpretable than a one-seam barrier trim. It should stay queued behind the compact final-drain experiment.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: If revisited later, the target remains compact-surface latency hiding and per-tile copy cadence.
candidates.risk: High. This family still carries shared-memory and pipeline-structure risk relative to the accepted compact anchor.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the port implies another shared-budget increase or changes the accepted compact dispatch in the same round as another sync tweak.
expected_local_changes: Keep the compact PTX dispatch active.
Only port the 2-K-stage preload and drain structure if the shared-memory budget remains tightly controlled.
Avoid coupling the port with another compact sync-family change.

candidates.direction_name: Leave The Wait-Sync-Collapse Family Parked Behind Smaller Compact Tweaks
ranking_notes: This family stays in the tree for completeness, but it no longer deserves immediate selection.
It ranks third because the last two concrete variants both regressed from near-base anchors.
Only revisit it after the smaller compact barrier trims and the guarded 2-K-stage family have been sampled.
candidates.hypothesis: The wait_sync_collapse family is still conceptually related to the bottleneck, but two recent near-base losses mean it should remain parked until the smaller compact-surface trims are exhausted.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: If revisited later, the target would again be the compact loop's wait/refill seam, but not before smaller barrier trims flatten out.
candidates.risk: Moderate-high. The family is clearly fragile on the current anchor and no longer deserves another immediate retry.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if revisiting this family would simply replay the refill-before-wait or wait_group_1 patterns that already lost.
expected_local_changes: Keep the family parked unless smaller compact-surface trims fully flatten out.
Do not replay the refill-before-wait or wait_group_1 variants already measured on this anchor.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
